title: Budh Chalisa
intro: Shri Budh Chalisa: Full Path, Meaning, and Benefits - Hymn to Lord Budh
description:
|| Shri Budh Dev Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Namo namo jay Shri Budh Raja. Karahun kripa mohi jaani kayar adham ka.||
Karahun kripa kripanidhi Budh sada sahay. Rog dosh dukh haro anath ke nath.||

|| Chaupai ||
Jayati jayati Budh dev dayala. Sada karat jo sukrit pratipala.||
Jata mukut sir shobhit bhari. Tripund chandan rekha pyari.||
Garal kanth sarp jag mala. Nag kankan kar mandit bhala.||
Brahma roop var shubhra sarira. Karat sada jan kalyan adhira.||
Shwet kamal aasan man bhava. Sant karat sada mangal dhyava.||
Kunjal birajat chhavi nayani. Ati manohar mangal gun khani.||
Katat patak pank bharara. Budh grah dusht narak sansara.||
Sukh srukhavat sab phal sata. Rog dosh sankat haran vidhata.||
Budh ki mahima aparapara. Kiya jaani manuj dukh nivara.||
Lakh ke vachan dharat dar sata. Rog haran Budh daya vihata.||
Grah anishta jo nar par chhae. Rog dosh bhay mitai nahin jae.||
Tinh par Buddh anugrah hoi. Kati dai sab sankat mohe.||
Janam janam ke patak bhari. Kati dai sab Budh mati tari.||
Sur nar muni nitya gun gave. Yash gavat Budh sukh pave.||
Rog dosh sankat sab hari. Dharahun dhir Budh harahu paap bhari.||
Nit nav mangal karat savari. Rog dosh Budh harahu bhari.||
Adham kayar matihin hamara. Karahun kripa Budh haro dukh sara.||
Sukh sampatti dai karahun upai. Jan man ranjan mangal lai.||
Budh sudhi seel roop suhava. Sant dhyavat mangal bhava.||
Vinay karau Budh dev tumhari. Sankat haro he patak bhari.||
Adham kayar subuddhi sudhara. Karahun kripa haro dukh sara.||
Maha sankat mein tinhen ubaro. Adham kayar subuddhi sudharo.||
Harahun paap Budh maha vidhata. Sur nar muni sada shubh gata.||
Budh ki mahima apar pave. Adham kayar sab sankat hare.||
Jayati jayati Budh dev sahay. Kripa karahun harahun sab bhay.||

|| Doha ||
Namo namo jay Budh sukh kari. Dukh daridrya mitao bhari.||
Yeh Chalisa Budh grah ka path. Karahun kripa Budh haro sab kasht.||

|| Samapt ||
meaning:
|| Shri Budh Dev Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Namo namo jay Shri Budh Raja. Karahun kripa mohi jaani kayar adham ka.||
(Salutations again and again to you, O King Budh. Please shower your grace upon me, considering me cowardly and low.)
Karahun kripa kripanidhi Budh sada sahay. Rog dosh dukh haro anath ke nath.||
(O Ocean of Mercy, Budh, always help me. O Lord of the helpless, remove all my diseases, faults, and sorrows.)

|| Chaupai ||
Jayati jayati Budh dev dayala. Sada karat jo sukrit pratipala.||
(Victory to you, O merciful Budh Dev, you who always uphold good deeds.)
Jata mukut sir shobhit bhari. Tripund chandan rekha pyari.||
(A crown of matted hair adorns your head, and a lovely Tripund sandal mark rests on your forehead.)
Garal kanth sarp jag mala. Nag kankan kar mandit bhala.||
(You wear a garland of serpents around your throat, and your hands are adorned with snake bracelets.)
Brahma roop var shubhra sarira. Karat sada jan kalyan adhira.||
(You are in the form of Brahma with a radiant white body. You always work for the welfare of the distressed.)
Shwet kamal aasan man bhava. Sant karat sada mangal dhyava.||
(Your seat on the white lotus is pleasing to the mind. Saints always meditate on your auspicious form.)
Kunjal birajat chhavi nayani. Ati manohar mangal gun khani.||
(Your beautiful eyes possess a divine image. You are extremely charming and a mine of auspicious qualities.)
Katat patak pank bharara. Budh grah dusht narak sansara.||
(You cut through the mire of sins. As the planet Budh, you save us from the hellish miseries of this world.)
Sukh srukhavat sab phal sata. Rog dosh sankat haran vidhata.||
(You provide all fruits of happiness. You are the creator who removes all diseases, faults, and crises.)
Budh ki mahima aparapara. Kiya jaani manuj dukh nivara.||
(The glory of Budh is boundless. Knowing the sorrows of humans, you remove them.)
Lakh ke vachan dharat dar sata. Rog haran Budh daya vihata.||
(By your grace, diseases are destroyed, and you listen to the cries of your devotees.)
Grah anishta jo nar par chhae. Rog dosh bhay mitai nahin jae.||
(When inauspicious planets loom over a person, and diseases, faults, and fears do not leave.)
Tinh par Buddh anugrah hoi. Kati dai sab sankat mohe.||
(On those upon whom Budh showers his grace, you cut away all their crises.)
Janam janam ke patak bhari. Kati dai sab Budh mati tari.||
(You destroy the heavy sins of many births and uplift the intellect.)
Sur nar muni nitya gun gave. Yash gavat Budh sukh pave.||
(Gods, humans, and sages daily sing your praises. By singing your glory, one attains happiness.)
Rog dosh sankat sab hari. Dharahun dhir Budh harahu paap bhari.||
(You remove all diseases, faults, and crises. Please grant me patience, O Budh, and remove my heavy sins.)
Nit nav mangal karat savari. Rog dosh Budh harahu bhari.||
(You always create new auspicious beginnings. O Budh, remove my heavy diseases and faults.)
Adham kayar matihin hamara. Karahun kripa Budh haro dukh sara.||
(I am cowardly, low, and mindless. O Budh, please shower your grace and remove all my sorrows.)
Sukh sampatti dai karahun upai. Jan man ranjan mangal lai.||
(Grant me happiness and wealth, and bring auspiciousness to please the minds of the people.)
Budh sudhi seel roop suhava. Sant dhyavat mangal bhava.||
(Your form is wise, virtuous, and pleasing. Saints meditate on your auspicious form.)
Vinay karau Budh dev tumhari. Sankat haro he patak bhari.||
(O Budh Dev, I pray to you. Remove my crises and heavy sins.)
Adham kayar subuddhi sudhara. Karahun kripa haro dukh sara.||
(Improve the intellect of this cowardly and low person. Shower your grace and remove all my sorrows.)
Maha sankat mein tinhen ubaro. Adham kayar subuddhi sudharo.||
(Save me from great crises and improve my cowardly intellect.)
Harahun paap Budh maha vidhata. Sur nar muni sada shubh gata.||
(Remove my sins, O great Creator Budh. Gods, humans, and sages always sing of your auspicious nature.)
Budh ki mahima apar pave. Adham kayar sab sankat hare.||
(Whoever attains the boundless glory of Budh, all their crises are removed.)
Jayati jayati Budh dev sahay. Kripa karahun harahun sab bhay.||
(Victory to you, O helper Budh Dev. Please shower your grace and remove all my fears.)

|| Doha ||
Namo namo jay Budh sukh kari. Dukh daridrya mitao bhari.||
(Salutations again to the bringer of happiness, Budh. Remove my great sorrows and poverty.)
Yeh Chalisa Budh grah ka path. Karahun kripa Budh haro sab kasht.||
(Whoever reads this Chalisa of planet Budh, please shower your grace, O Budh, and remove all their troubles.)

|| Samapt ||

faq.q1: Why is the Budh Chalisa recited?
faq.a1: The planet Mercury (Budh) is the significator of intellect, logic, communication, and business. This Chalisa is recited to enhance concentration, overcome speech-related issues, and attain success in education and business.
faq.q2: What is the most auspicious day to recite it?
faq.a2: Wednesday is dedicated to the worship of Budh Dev. Therefore, reciting it on a Wednesday is considered the most beneficial.
faq.q3: Is the Budh Chalisa useful for students?
faq.a3: Yes, Mercury is the lord of intellect. For students, regular recitation of this Chalisa helps in improving focus, enhancing memory, and performing better in exams.
faq.a4: Absolutely. For those who feel confused in making business decisions or lack effective communication skills in their work, this Chalisa is very effective.
faq.q5: Is any special material required for this?
faq.a5: There are no mandatory requirements, but items associated with the color green are significant for Budh Dev. If possible, offering green Moong dal or green items as 'Bhog' is considered very auspicious.
